links.targetTitle: The adapter writes manifests as 32-byte references inside SOC feeds, not inline payloads

global.title: Manifests are uploaded encrypted to /bytes and only the 64-char hash is written to the feed
global.content:
Rather than inlining large manifest JSON into a feed chunk, Connect uses a regenerate-and-publish pattern:

1. Serialize the manifest to a JSON string.
2. Encrypt it with AES-256-GCM → encrypted bytes.
3. Upload to `/bytes` → receive a 64-char content hash.
4. Write ONLY the hash to the feed → a 72-byte SOC (8-byte timestamp + 64-byte reference).

Reading reverses the flow: read the feed to get the hash, download the encrypted bytes from `/bytes`, decrypt, and parse JSON.

This pattern keeps feed writes fast (tiny SOC payloads) and is why feeds are a cheap mutable index over large, otherwise-immutable data. It applies uniformly to document, drive, and user manifests.

The `SwarmClient` also auto-detects legacy feeds: a feed that contains inline JSON instead of a 64-char hex reference is read with the legacy path for backward compatibility.
global.description: The regenerate-and-publish pattern keeps feed SOCs tiny (8-byte timestamp + 64-byte reference = 72 bytes) and moves manifest size/latency to /bytes where it belongs.
